plan: Reduce SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce broad market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk and meet the mandate.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and broad index exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Trim CRWD to 10% to manage single name concentration.
Buy TSLA at 5% as a high-quality name with a strong scanner setup.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but also limits potential upside. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF risk.
Increase QQQI to 22% for a balanced income and broad market exposure.
Trim SMH to 10% to reduce semiconductor sector concentration.
Add to CRWD to 15% to lean into a high-scoring tech name.
Buy TSLA at 8% as a strong, high-quality setup.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while still allowing for growth from quality tech. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Trim SOXL to 5% to maintain some leveraged exposure within limits.
Sell SPAL to remove an underperforming leveraged ETF.
Increase CRWD to 18% to concentrate in a high-scoring tech name.
Buy TSLA at 10% to add a high-scoring, high-beta name.
Buy NVDU at 7% to add a high risk/reward scanner setup.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It uses leverage and concentrates in high-scoring setups, which significantly raises both potential upside and drawdown risk. Be aware that leveraged ETFs decay daily, and this approach can lead to substantial losses quickly if stops are not strictly adhered to.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize income-focused exposure.
Sell CRWD to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Trim SMH to 5% to reduce semiconductor sector exposure.
Maintain a significant cash buffer to prioritize low drawdown and stability.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, focusing on steady monthly distributions. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth by rotating into income/covered-call ETFs and broad indices. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is not a savings account but aims for cash-like income.
